title: Fermented Creamy Malaysian Potatoes Curry
description: A quick, dairyfree Malaysian-style curry with new potatoes simmered in coconut milk and fermented coconut yogurt for a tangy, creamy finish.

ingredients.name: Spice blend
ingredients.notes: fresh grated ginger, fresh grated galangal, sliced red chili, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground coriander, 1 tsp turmeric powder

instructions: Cut the new potatoes into 1‑inch cubes and rinse under cold water.
Heat the peanut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and bruised lemongrass, sautéing for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the spice blend and cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly to release the aromas.
Pour in the coconut milk and stir in the palm sugar and tamarind paste. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the cubed potatoes to the sauce, reduce the heat to low, cover, and let simmer for 15‑20 minutes or until the potatoes are tender.
Once the potatoes are cooked, remove the saucepan from heat and stir in the fermented coconut yogurt until well combined. Adjust salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the curry to serving bowl(s). Sprinkle crushed roasted peanuts on top and garnish with chopped fresh cilantro. Serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread.
